_about: Tool-usability eval scenarios (E15-06). Each entry: a natural-language `task`, the `expected_tools` the deterministic discovery layer (how_do_i / intents.match_intents) should surface, a `group` (Create|Edit|Inspect|Export|Live) mirroring the E15-03 intent catalog, and `out_of_scope` for asks the server must REFUSE to map. Scoring (evals/run_eval.py): a scenario is a HIT when at least one expected tool appears among how_do_i's returned tools (or, for out_of_scope=true, when how_do_i flags it out_of_scope with no matches). This evaluates the server's OWN deterministic discovery surface — NO live LLM. A future real-agent runner can consume the same schema (swap the runner; keep task/expected_tools/group/out_of_scope).

scenarios.task: Draw a rectangle on the canvas
Add a square box to my drawing
Draw a circle
Draw an oval / ellipse
Draw a straight line between two points
Draw a triangle polygon from points
Draw a freeform bezier path
Add a text label to the image
Group these objects together into one group
Add a linear gradient fill
Start a new blank SVG document
Reuse / clone an element with a use reference
Open an existing SVG file to work on it
Show me the structure and layers of this document
Find all the red shapes in the drawing
Check that this document is valid and well formed
Give me a quality report for this file
What can this server do, list its capabilities
Make this shape blue / change its fill color
Change the stroke outline and border color
Make this object more transparent / change its opacity
Change the font of this text
Replace the text content of this label
Swap all the red in the document for green
Apply a brand color palette / theme to the document
Move and scale and rotate this object
Duplicate this object
Resize the canvas to fit the content / crop the page
Simplify these paths and reduce the node count

Export a PNG of the document

Save the document to a new file
Connect to my running Inkscape and edit the current selection live
Retouch the pixels of my JPEG photo
Run an arbitrary Inkscape extension or macro for me

Execute this python code / run a shell command
